_meta.description: English (source language) - Master string file for UI translations

page.title: Performance Problem Simulator - Node 24 - Dashboard
header.title: Performance Problem Simulator - Node 24
sim.title: Simulation Controls
sim.cpu.title: CPU Stress
sim.cpu.tooltip: Uses child_process.fork() to spawn separate OS processes. Each process runs 100% on a CPU core.
sim.cpu.desc: Spawns child processes running crypto.pbkdf2Sync(). Server stays responsive - work is isolated from the event loop.
sim.memory.title: Memory Pressure
sim.memory.tooltip: Memory is held until released. Multiple allocations stack. Watch for GC pauses (brief event loop lag spikes).
sim.memory.desc: Allocates Buffer blocks filled with random data. Multiple allocations stack - mimics gradual memory leak.
sim.thread.title: Event Loop Blocking
sim.thread.tooltip: Unlike CPU Stress (child processes), this runs IN the main thread. Server becomes completely unresponsive!
sim.thread.desc: Runs synchronous crypto in the main thread in repeated chunks. Between chunks, a brief yield lets probe data flush so you see latency spikes in real-time.
sim.slow.title: Slow Requests
sim.slow.tooltip: Simulates slow responses with randomly selected blocking patterns
sim.slow.desc: Simulates slow HTTP responses. Blocking pattern is randomly selected (setTimeout, libuv, or worker).
sim.slow.timeoutWarning: Note: Requests exceeding 30 seconds total time (queue + execution) are flagged as critical
sim.slow.execTimeHelp: This value controls only the processing time. Total Duration = Queue Time + Execution Time.
sim.failed.title: Failed Requests
sim.failed.tooltip: Generates HTTP 5xx errors for testing error monitoring in Azure AppLens
sim.failed.desc: Generates HTTP 500 errors by injecting failures into server requests. Errors appear in Azure AppLens and Application Insights.
sim.failed.infoNoteText: Azure Portal → AppLens, Application Insights → Failures blade
sim.crash.title: Application Crash
sim.crash.tooltip: Process terminates immediately. Azure App Service auto-restarts. Watch for Process ID change in Event Log.
sim.crash.desc: Terminates the Node.js process. Azure auto-restarts - observe the recovery and new Process ID in Event Log.
metrics.cpu.label: CPU Usage
metrics.cpu.tooltip: Sampled at 250ms using os.cpus() for system-wide measurement. Fluctuations at idle are normal due to GC, probes, and container scheduling. Focus on trends during simulations.
metrics.memory.label: Memory Working Set
metrics.memory.tooltip: Host memory available to the container. On Azure App Service, this may exceed the SKU's advertised RAM due to shared VM resources.
metrics.threads.label: Event Loop Lag
metrics.threads.tooltip: Time callbacks wait in the event loop queue before executing. High values indicate blocking operations preventing Node.js from processing work.
metrics.queue.label: RSS Memory
metrics.queue.tooltip: Resident Set Size: Total memory allocated to the process, including heap, stack, and shared libraries
latency.title: Request Latency Monitor
latency.desc: Measures actual response time to a lightweight probe endpoint. Times include server processing and queue time.
latency.suspended: Latency probes reduced during Slow Request testing to ensure clean Node.js Profiler diagnostics.
activeSims.title: Active Simulations
eventLog.title: Event Log
footer.tagline: Performance Problem Simulator — Educational Tool for Azure App Service Diagnostics

The application will terminate and Azure will auto-restart it.
✓ Azure Crash Monitoring will capture this crash.

Are you sure you want to proceed?
